TextENGNew: "...I'm a bit late, huh?"
Tomoe wiped the sweat from her forehead with a handkerchief and caught her breath, while checking her watch in front of the meeting place.
Despite moving at a brisk pace, it took at least 30 minutes to walk here from the station.
She did hear it was far away, so maybe she would've been better off taking a taxi instead of being so stingy.
"Anyway, this place even has a location in Kakiuchi.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1043. Not sure if I should be disgusted or impressed..."
She lightly frowned, then took a good long look at the store with a bitter smile on her face.
Maid cafe restaurant, Angel Mort.
Famous for its colorful costumes and vibrant sweets, this restaurant chain now had locations in urban areas all across the country.

But her first visit was at the Tokyo location with her former boss, Yamaoki. She was so overwhelmed by the strange atmosphere that she shouted from the bottom of her heart "I'm never, ever coming back!" ...Or so she thought.

Nevertheless, ever since that day Tomoe was completely fascinated by the taste of the sweets the store had to offer. She had been using her loyalty card to rack up a fair number of points up until today.
No, the truth is... when she pulled her membership card out of her jacket pocket, it was a dazzlingly gold color.
Calling it a fair number of points was a major understatement. Not even close.
Additionally, since female customers are something of a rarity, the waitresses at the Tokyo store recognize her as a "regular customer" the moment they see her.
Although... there is another, more intense reason they recognize her...
"...Whoops, can't just stand around here."
Tomoe remembered her meeting and quickly ran up the stairs. Then, she opened the door and stepped inside.
"Welcome home, milady♪@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_waitFa1002. Are you here alone?"
"Oh, no.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1047. I'm meeting someone here..."
As she said that, she looked over the shoulder of the waitress greeting her to check the shop... and a little ways from the back, she noticed a raised hand.
When Tomoe saw that, she turned and smiled, then waited for the waitress's permission before heading to the table.
"Sorry I'm late.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1049. Did I keep you waiting?"
"No, not at all.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1106. I just got here, myself."
The girl sitting on the far side of the table... Natsumi shook her head and smiled happily.
...Despite what she said, the orange juice sitting in front of her was half-empty, and the ice was completely melted.
(She's still as humble as ever.)
Tomoe took a seat across from her, and suddenly let out a chuckle as she looked at the glass.
Still... compared to when they first met, when she didn't order anything at their meetings and just drank water, it seemed like she was feeling a little less reserved.
Thinking that... brought her a little happiness.
"...?@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1108. Is something wrong?"
"No, not really.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1052. ...Anyway, this conversation is starting to sound like a date.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1053. And between two women. It's a little embarrassing."
"Ahaha, yeah.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1110. ...Oh, would you like something to drink?"
"Hmm, let me think.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1055. It'd be nice to have something sweet, since it's been a while.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1056. Excuuuse me."
Tomoe raised her hand and called out to a waitress standing nearby.
Then, the waitress came running with a smile on her face and spread the menu out in front of her, and Tomoe circled the item in the righthand corner of the menu with her finger.
"Umm, about this all-you-can-eat ladies course... is that still available?"
"Yes!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_waitFb1002. All items are available at this location."
"Then, two orders of that please.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1059. For now, just bring all of the cakes on this page."
"Okay, understood, mila,@k@|@y@vS14/00/VT4Bb_waitFb1004. ...dy?!"
"Oh, don't worry. You don't have to bring them all at once.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1061. And I'd like a hot coffee to go with it."
"O...of course, r-right away...!"
The waitress's eyes opened wide in disbelief at Tomoe's order. Nevertheless, she bowed and headed back to the kitchen to fulfill her duties.
Natsumi let out an amused giggle as she watched the waitress go.
"You're as incredible as ever...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1112. Meanwhile, I feel full after just a single cake."

"Mmm, well, it's just because I'm really active, and my job requires me to constantly use my head.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1063. They say it's not very ladylike, but if I held back on things like this I'd get less joy out of life."
"Ahahaha."
Meanwhile, back in the kitchen... there was a huge commotion like the place was being turned upside down.
"Ch-chief?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_waitFc1002. ...Could it be? That woman is...?"
"...Yes, no doubt about it.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_waitFa1004. That's "Miss Quadruple" from the Tokyo store!"
"Wha?!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_waitFc1004. Th-the Legendary Food Queen who managed to eat four of every item on the menu at the Tokyo shop last winter?!"
All the staff suddenly turned and gawked at the faxed picture from the head office hanging on the wall.
It was a close-up black and white photo of a woman with a vague smile on her face, but it felt more like a wanted poster you'd see on a police box.
...And below that was a caption written in thick red marker, "Beware!"

"...B-but the Tokyo location had full-size cakes intended for parties on the menu back then, right?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_waitFb1007. Those are too big for even a party of ten to finish!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_waitFb1008. And... she ate four of those?!"
"Even if we exclude those, there are over 20 varieties of parfait on the menu, right?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_waitFc1006. Four times that would be... U-ugh...?!"
"D-don't try to imagine it!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_waitFa1006. It'll end up haunting you in your dreams!"
"P-plus!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_waitFb1010. I heard they cancelled the Food Battle at the Kanto spring Dessert Festa after she won the winter fest in all three categories: Volume, speed, and connoisseur?!"
"And I hear that every single one of the boys who tried to challenge her ended up carried out to an ambulance...!"
Unfortunately, none of the staff at the Kakiuchi location actually witnessed the scene.
However... the people who had witnessed it described it as a "tragedy" and turned pale any time they spoke of it.
"...In the first place, I've never met someone dressed in red who turned out to be any good."
"I'm sure that's what a black hole looks like as it's devouring asteroids and planets..."
"It's not like she had an iron stomach, or her whole body was a stomach, or something cheap like that.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_cusC1002. What we caught a glimpse of is something far, far more terrifying..."
...They'd barely believed any of those words.
No... that wasn't it. They didn't want to believe it.
Because much like how sumo wrestlers and macho supermen were concepts that didn't belong together... "lady" and "glutton" were concepts that should never be combined, riiiiiiight?!
"E-even if it's all part of a legitimate business transaction... in a way, it's far more disruptive than a chronic complainer...!"

"A-anyway!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_waitFa1008. Call in every patissier on break!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_waitFa1009. We'll have to pull a scramble shift on the same scale as Christmas sales if we're going to survive this!"
"U-understood!!"
By tomorrow, Tomoe would be "famous"... or rather, "infamous" at the Kakiuchi location.
Nevertheless...

"Whyyyy did you have to leave something as dangerous as that "ladies all-you-can-eat" item on the menu?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_waitFa1011. That's like a duck strolling up to a pot holding a leek and covered in misoooo?!"
The head chef cursed the store manager for completely failing to understand the horrifying notion that women have "a second stomach for desserts..."
...Beyond the hustle and bustle in the kitchen.
Tomoe and Natsumi were having an enjoyable chat full of friendly atmosphere.
"You really look so different.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1065. If you hadn't waved your hand, I probably wouldn't have noticed you for a while."
"Y-you're exaggerating, Tomoe-san."
"Ahahaha!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1067. More importantly, how did it go?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1068. Was everyone surprised to see your hair?"
"Yeah, super surprised!@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1116. And, Chisato-chan and everyone else told me it looked "nice" and it "really suited me"..."

"Hehe... See? I told you it would be okay.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1070. The straight and long style has been an icon of beauty since ancient times.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1071. Do you have a little more faith in my judgment now?"
"E-ehehe..."
Natsumi laughed and nodded while bashfully running her fingers through her hair.
Tomoe had told her from the start it would be all right, but she was worried about how the class might react, so those responses brought her that much more joy.
"Hey, Natsumi-san.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1073. ...Has your world changed?"
"Does the world seen through your eyes look a little brighter?"
"...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1120.Yes."
Natsumi tightly held her hand against her chest and firmly nodded back to her.
Even thought it was already night time, there was something dazzlingly bright in her field of view... and it wasn't just a trick of the lighting.
That's right. ...It was just a few days ago that Natsumi decided to change up her hairstyle.

On the way back from her part-time job, while taking shelter in front of Kakiuchi station during a sudden rain shower, she happened to run into Tomoe who was in the middle of heading to the police station. And their conversation at the time was what triggered it...
"...Oh? Natsumi-san?"
"Oh... G-good afternoon."
"Hehe...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1077. Guess you were fooled by the morning weather forecast, too?"
"...Ah, yeah."
Natsumi reproachfully looked up at the gloomy sky as she answered.
The sky was so dark and stagnant that it looked like it was almost nightfall, but it was probably just the rain. If they waited a little while, it would probably let up.
...But the rain started well before she was able to take shelter under the roof, so her wet clothes and hair were uncomfortably clinging to her skin.
She tried to wipe herself down with a handkerchief, but it was so small it quickly became soggy and useless.
That moment...
A soft object was suddenly draped over her head, blocking her view. ...It was a towel.
And when she lightly moved it out of her eyes, she found Tomoe there, smiling at her.
"Tomoe-san..."

"Here, you can borrow this.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1079. I'm a pretty sweaty person, so handkerchiefs don't always get the job done.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1080. ...Oh, don't worry, this one hasn't been used yet."
"...Th-thanks."

Thinking it would be rude to refuse the favor at this point, Natsumi shrugged her shoulders and offered her thanks. And the truth is, she really did think she'd catch a cold if she remained the way she was.
"Haah..."
Tomoe's towel was surprisingly absorbent, like something designed for athletes. A fragrant scent tickled her nose and made her feel very comfortable.
(This... isn't detergent.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1128. What is this scent...?)
Thinking that, and while wiping her hair down like she was combing it... Natsumi suddenly caught her finger on a knot.
And to make it worse, her elastic hair tie came out in the process, causing her bun to come undone and her carefully tied hair to spill over and fall down.
She tried to set it back up in a hurry... but it usually takes several minutes to tie it in the morning, and it would be especially difficult right now since she didn't have a mirror.
"...?@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1131. Umm, what is it?"
"Oh, sorry.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1083. This is the first time I've seen you with your hair down, Natsumi-san.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1084. ...You have very beautiful hair."
"Huh?@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1133. Y-you think so...?"
"Yeah.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1086. It's smooth and glossy... You spend a lot of time washing your hair, don't you?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1087. It wouldn't look that nice without consistent treatment."
"...Ah, thank you."
Embarrassed by Tomoe's impressions, Natsumi suddenly looked away to hide her burning cheeks. ...Even if it was flattering, she couldn't quite handle being called beautiful.
"And it's hard to notice when it's wrapped in buns, but it's actually quite long.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1089. I think it would be rather cute to leave it straight and flowing."
"?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1091. What's wrong, Natsumi-san?"
For some reason... Natsumi suddenly turned her gaze downward and started looking gloomy.
Sensing the discomfort in that reaction, Tomoe ran through her words again in her head, wondering if she has said something weird. But before she could say anything, Natsumi muttered a response.
"...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1138.I... used to leave my hair down like this before.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1139. But..."
"In my old town... I got a bad nickname.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1141. They said my long hair made me look like a ghost... so they called me "Zashiki-warashi.""
"Zashiki-warashi? ...But isn't that a ghost with a bob cut?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1094. I don't see the resemblance at all."
Tomoe couldn't help but laugh, amazed by their poor naming sense and vocabulary. ...Although, most of the nicknames children come up with are completely baseless.
"Haah... At least say it looks like a snow fairy, or something.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1096. Or maybe... Snow White? No, that doesn't work. Ahaha."

"...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1143.But... I was really gloomy back then, and not as good at talking with people...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1144. So, I wasn't strong enough to tell them to stop, and so the name stuck after that..."
"...I see.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1098. Were you still in elementary school back then?"
Natsumi remained silent and nodded. ...Upon hearing that, Tomoe recalled that she had gone through a similar experience of her own, then stopped smiling and clicked her tongue.
(Sheesh... There are stupid boys in every generation, huh?)
Young children before puberty, especially the boys, have a tendency to tease girls and make fun of them.
The ones who mature fastest, look the most stylish, or have long hair are most likely to be targeted. ...Essentially, the ones who show the strongest sexual dimorphism.
In many cases, it's like the flip side of "shyness" that emanates from some form of longing. It's nothing more than an act of wanting to make people pay attention to you.
...However, in some cases their mental state is far too underdeveloped, so they take it too far and end up deeply hurting their target.
And, of course, the listener will over-interpret it and take it to heart... which can have a negative effect on their interpersonal development.
(I'm sure... she went through that, too.)
Tomoe had known for a while now that Natsumi was a particularly sensitive girl.
And so, given that personality, it was especially likely that unpleasant past memories could remain traumatizing even as she grew older. And that could develop into a type of inferiority complex...
"...Such mean-spirited brats.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1102. If I was in your shoes, I'd definitely beat the shi...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1103. I mean, I'd knock some sense into them."
"Ahahaha...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1146. Actually, I'm not very good at tying my hair, so leaving it down would be nice.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1147. ...But ever since then, I've had a bad image of it."
"I know, it would be okay to just ignore it.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1149. Compared to someone as self-confident as you, Tomoe-san... I must look pathetic.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1150. I'm too weak..."
"Natsumi-san..."
She's had a hard life. ...Tomoe couldn't help but mutter that to herself.
She a gentle, quiet, and adorable girl.
...In fact, it was no exaggeration to say that she had all the qualifications necessary to call her a "beautiful girl."
Nevertheless, all those aspects of her beauty actually had a negative impact on her in her childhood... that endured even to the present day.
Since she's modest, she won't talk back to her aggressors in a fierce tone.
Since she's kind, she lets all her discontent accumulate within herself.
And since she's diligent and obedient, she looks for the reasoning behind the things they said, which actually makes it difficult for her to escape that negative mindset.
As a result, she continues to be dragged down by past slander, and her lack of self-confidence persists even now...
(So, that corrupted her down to her core, huh...)
Tomoe gently sighed while watching the flow of people folding up their umbrellas as they passed through the ticket gate.
It's easy to tell someone to cheer up when you're in a better position.
At the very least, if you lightly smile and talk down the problem, the surrounding atmosphere will return to normal... and the conversation will end there.
But, Tomoe shook her head. ...At this rate, Natsumi would continue to carry these worries for a long time coming.
(...I can't just leave it at that.)
...She didn't need anyone saying anything to know that might be considered meddlesome.
But, for someone in that state all they need is the time to come to a decision, and someone who can trigger it.
Although, it might be arrogant and presumptuous to believe that she was in that position right now...
(...It's fine.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1109. Meddling it is.)
Having come to a decision, Tomoe gently stroked Natsumi's head as she continued hiding her face behind the towel.
And, when the girl looked up with a dubious expression, Tomoe slowly spoke up.
"Natsumi-san.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1111. ...Actually, I'm envious."
"You know what sort of personality I have.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1113. My hands are faster than my mouth, so I got into fights with the boys all the time."

"Plus, with my height like this, and my chest... well, like this.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1115. I was rather jealous of my classmates who looked good in a one-piece, so there were times I felt disheartened by my lack of femininity.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1116. ...I actually felt really depressed about it."
"...Really, Tomoe-san?"

"Of course, I was a maiden in my own way back in my school days.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1118. There were people I had crushes on... and I got flat out rejected after confessing to them, countless times."

"So, I started asking myself. How do I become more pretty? How can I make myself more appealing to men?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1120. But looking back on it now, I want to laugh at those problems... I tried to do all sorts of impossible things to stretch myself beyond my limits."
"...But, even so."
Tomoe shrugged her shoulders with a bitter smile on her face, and spoke in as soft a tone as she could muster.
"I can't be you, and you can't be me."

"A cutesy girl, or a graceful lady... Even now, I still feel a bit of a yearning to be like that, but unfortunately, that's just not me.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1124. So, at some point I had a breakthrough... and realized the best thing is to simply be yourself."
"Be yourself...?"

"Yes.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1126. To put it plainly, continue living life as it naturally comes to you, accept whatever praise you receive from others, and do your best to believe in yourself and let that confidence show."
"Of course, do try to fix any shortcoming that can make other people feel uncomfortable... but if you're overly self-conscious of that, you're more likely to hide those flaws rather than fix them."
..."Fixing" versus "hiding" your shortcomings.
Those two responses are very similar, and the latter can often be achieved without the host even realizing it.
If you don't want to be told you're a poor speaker, simply don't join the conversation in the first place.
If you don't want people pointing out that you're weak willed, don't interact with people at all.
...But that act won't eliminate or mitigate your shortcomings. You're actually missing out on the opportunity to do so.
And you can't let others see that you're "hiding" how you feel, so that shame casts a shadow on your heart, leading to a real possibility of developing an inferiority complex or self-loathing.
"Fixing it... isn't the same as hiding it..."
"Right.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1129. And I think I realized that because I looked at myself and was able to see both the good and the bad."

"Anyway, being stubborn and strong-minded... may not have earned me many points as a woman, but other people told me it was a good thing, so I decided to believe them.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1131. It felt easy, and natural."

"And then, I started to notice those shortcomings while doing that, and little by little I tried to fix them so I'd be less of an annoyance to others.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1133. ...And that's who I am now."
Natsumi suddenly looked back on herself, with Tomoe's words circling over and over inside her head.
How...
How had she been facing herself up until now?
No, had she ever really faced herself at all?
...She was always conscious of the "shortcomings" that made things difficult for others, and that her parents pointed out to her.
And she had been trying over and over to improve them through trial and error.
Were the things she did actually "fixing" things?
Her gloomy personality. Her melancholy appearance. Her lack of special skills to entertain others, and general lack of conversation ability.
She spent the whole time trying to "hide" them rather than "fix" them.
So... she was scared of other people learning about that.
She worried those flaws would inadvertently emerge some time.
That guilt and hesitation made her feel inferior... and it weighed heavily on her heart.
"...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1159.But."
"Hmm?"
"I... can't think of it that way.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1161. Tomoe-san... you're able to say that because you're strong."
"...Natsumi-san."
"I'm sorry if this is rude of me to say.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1163. But, I... can't be that rational, and I can't believe in myself like that."
"I can't think of anything good about myself, and people only point out the bad things, so I can't just brush it off like it's nothing.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1165. So, I... can't be like you."
"...I understand that.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1167. I... understand that well..."
Natsumi gently looked down and muttered that, with the towel still on her head.
...Even she knew it was miserable thing to say. But she couldn't help saying it.
A strong person... with the confidence to take on anything, and a sense of superiority over others, can easily overcome pain and hardship.
But... for those who don't, the mere act of "making an effort" is painful and trying.
Their circumstances were completely different, so someone like Tomoe who was excellent at everything couldn't understand her feelings.
She sincerely looking out for Natsumi. ...But as long as there was that huge disparity between them, she was ultimately just imposing her own selfish values on the girl.
...However.
"...Ahaha."
"Natsumi-san, didn't you notice?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1138. Just now, you said the same thing I did."
"Huh?@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1170. Ah..."
Natsumi suddenly looked up in response to Tomoe's laughter, and began to blush as she thought about the words she just said.
...It was just as she said. Though the emotions behind them were the complete opposite, the actual words were the same.
"It's like I said before... You can't be me, and the opposite also holds true.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1140. So, there's no need to try and be like me."
"Find your own opportunities to change, and find the means to do it yourself."

"I'm not talking about finding something completely new and changing yourself to suit it.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1143. It's about changing your perspective and way of thinking... just take a close-up view of some aspect of yourself, and moderately embellish it."
"Changing my... perspective and way of thinking...?"

"Right, exactly.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1145. Going from zero to one is hard, but taking something that's already present and thinking about how you can show it is much easier.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1146. And you do have nice qualities of your own that I lack, right?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1147. Like... your hair, for example?"
Tomoe gently rubbed her fingers through Natsumi's moist hair as she said that.
...Despite being tied up until a few minutes ago, her long dark hair was completely straight, and had a smooth feel in Tomoe's hand like it was bathed in oil.
"What about this is ghost-like?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1149. I think this hair is beautiful... Honestly, I'm so envious."
"Tomoe-san..."
"...But if you hadn't let it down today, I never would've known how great it is."

"It's probably the same way,@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1152. with the strong and weak points of your personality.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1153. That's why if you try to "hide" something you think is a shortcoming, you lose the opportunity to see that it might actually be an advantage."

"Plus, your values and impressions change with the time and place, so you can't look at things in a one-sided way.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1155. Strong and weak points can easily be flipped around from an outside viewer's perspective."

"After all, some people look at wildflowers and say they're beautiful, while others say they're just weeds, right?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1157. The same person can even have different impressions of the same thing depending on their emotional state at the time..."

"But the important thing isn't focusing on which of those is correct.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1159. It's about how the people close to you... the people you like think.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1160. ...Right?"
"...Yeah."
...Every word Tomoe said felt so warm.
It was different from the half-hearted lip service and obnoxious preaching she'd heard before. Tomoe was offering her own advice and encouragement because she genuinely cared about her.
There was a strong belief in there, full of trust in others.
And so, little by little, the stifling stagnation that had been accumulating in Natsumi's chest for a while now gradually disappeared. She felt the strength returning to her stiff neck and shoulders.

"Natsumi-san.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1162. I can't say what you think of yourself, but... I think you're kind, honest, and accepting.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1163. ...Those are all good qualities you should be proud of, qualities I don't have."
"At the very least, I think they're exceptional, and I love that about you.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1165. So, I want you to be confident and proud of yourself."
"?@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1178. Uh, umm..."
Natsumi bashfully turned her gaze downward when she heard the word "love" being said so directly. ...It was the first time someone said that straight to her face.
But... it wasn't bad at all. In fact, she was very, very happy to hear it.
Not only because someone older was showing her respect, but also because Tomoe was longing for something of Natsumi's that she didn't have...
Even though her heart shouldn't have gotten so riled up hearing the word "love" from a woman.
"Me too... Tomoe-san, you're always so good at speaking clearly... I think that's wonderful.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1180. I love you too, Tomoe-san. Honestly... I adore you."
"...Yeah, thanks."

"But, even if you tell me that, I don't really feel it at all. ...I can't believe it.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1182. I always try to be kind and I always want to make my friends happy, but when it doesn't work out... I feel sad and depressed..."
"So, I don't know... if I really possess all these nice qualities you say I have..."
"But, didn't your friends tell you?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1168. That they like those things about you, I mean.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1169. ...In which case, why not believe it?"
"It does indeed take a lot of courage to believe in yourself.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1171. I understand that well."
"...But the people you love tell you they like you for who you are, right?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1173. Then, why not relax a little more and let your natural self out?"
"My natural... self...?"

"Yes, that's right.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1175. And then, the shortcomings you've been keeping hidden... or rather, the things you've been led to believe are "shortcomings" can come out, and little by little you can double-check them."

"If it's actually something bad, you can talk it out and try to fix it.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1177. But, if it was a misunderstanding, then don't do anything about it.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1178. Do that, and I think you'll find yourself coming up with more positive solutions than when you were constantly hiding them."
Come to think of it, Natsumi remembered something.
When she confessed her honest feelings and worries to Chisato and Tamako... the two of them did, indeed, tell her.
"Relying on us is fine." "Being honest is fine."
They made a promise, "If any of us is making things difficult for each other, let's talk it out properly and fix the problem."
...She was delighted.
For Natsumi, who was starting to feel painfully crushed by the need to behave like a good child... those were practically the words of salvation.
But... honestly, she was still worried.
If she exposed the part of "herself" that she was hesitant to reveal and it got a poor reaction... what would she do?
And, if it makes them disappointed or uncomfortable... in the end, would they stop liking her and instead badmouth her like her old classmates did...?
It was frightening just imagining it... A chill ran through Natsumi that had nothing to do with her damp clothes, and her whole body began shaking.
"Hehe... That's no good, Natsumi-san.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1180. That's one of your weak points."
Perhaps sensing the musings in Natsumi's mind, Tomoe gently poked Natsumi on the nose with her finger. And then, she stared straight at the wide-eyed girl, as if to offer her guidance.

"The fact that you immediately start thinking about the feelings of others is a very good thing.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1182. ...But you can't just think that you're going to be rejected. You have to believe in the possibility that they'll accept you."
"So, don't go into it expecting to be a bother for them, and don't assume they'll say they don't like it... okay?"

"Plus, a close friend will be able to forgive anything in your heart, right?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1185. If you don't believe they'll handle it in good faith, then I feel bad for those friends.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1186. ...That's what kindness is."
...Right.
They're open-minded people, so why not trust that they're willing to accept you as you are?
There's a chance you'll be betrayed, and you may not meet their expectations, so it certainly is uneasy and scary. ...But it's important to have faith and take that first step.
"Oh, right.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1188. ...In that case... Natsumi-san?"
"Yes?"
"This is also a good opportunity.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1190. To foster your courage, how about we actually prove how completely nonsensical the bad things those kids said about you truly are?"
"Huh...?@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1192. Prove it...? How?"
"Isn't it obvious?!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1192. Revert to your original straight and long hairstyle!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1193. Because it absolutely does look good on you, okay?"
"I-I couldn't...!"
Natsumi was so startled she was about to fall over, and then she immediately began shaking her head.
...Tomoe's proposal was too big of a leap.
Seriously, what kind of reaction would she get if she suddenly underwent such a drastic makeover...?!
"I-I can't...!@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1195. That's too much."
"Are you still worried about being called Zashiki-warashi?"

"Who do you think you're kidding? Who ever heard of such a cute ghost?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1196. If someone actually does say that, you bring them straight to me!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1197. I'll drag them straight to an ophthalmologist and a psychiatrist!"

"It'll be all right, I guarantee it.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1199. Because you're super cute with straight hair, Natsumi-san.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1200. Everyone is definitely going to have the same thought, right?"
Natsumi ducked her head down in a hard rejection.
Tomoe stared at her like that for a while... but then, she suddenly pinched Natsumi's cheeks and lightly tugged at them.
"Whoa?!@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1199. Wh-what...?"
"...Natsumi-san.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1202. I told you earlier, you and I are different.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1203. ...But there's one thing we have in common."
"Huh?"
"We're surprisingly stubborn."
"S-stubb...?!"
Natsumi's thoughts ceased for a moment when she noticed that point of commonality between herself and Tomoe that she had never even considered before.
...If someone other than Tomoe had told her that, she might've taken it as an insult and felt resentful. Or maybe she'd start feeling depressed and cut off the conversation right then and there.

Even so, a person she loved and respected also pointing out that she had the same characteristic in herself... She wasn't sure if she was happy about it or not, but she couldn't help but have a strange feeling come over her.
"I-I'm... that stubborn?"
"Yeah, just as much as me.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1206. That's why you never cut your hair, right?"

"I'm sorry if this is baseless conjecture.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1208. But despite other people saying bad things about your hair, you've still taken very good care of it, even up until now.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1209. I mean... doesn't that prove that you, yourself, really thought that your hair was a good quality?"

"It's the same for me... I actually really like this strong-minded personality.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1211. So, even though people told me I ought to fix that about myself, I thought about it... and decided it wasn't something that needed to change."
"Don't you think the situation with your hair is similar?"
Natsumi's eyes opened wide when Tomoe pointed that out.
Because it wasn't mere conjecture... It was precisely that.
The truth is... she inherited this hair from her mother, and she liked it a lot. That's why she insisted on growing it out since she was young, and never cut it.
But nobody ever praised it, so she started to think it was nothing more than personal bias, and always kept quiet about it.
It became a secret feeling that she couldn't share with anyone. ...But Tomoe was able to spot it easily.
(That's right... she really is different.)
It was the first time that someone understood her... or rather, that someone truly made an effort to understand her.
Not even her parents had ever spoken to her that sincerely...
"...Tomoe-san, you really are amazing.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1208. No matter what I'm thinking, you always pick up on it immediately.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1209. You're a detective, all right."
"Hmm, well, I'm actually not a detective at the moment.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1214. ...Ahaha."

"My mom has really pretty hair, too...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1211. My relatives often tell her how nice it is.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1212. But... they've never told me that, so I thought maybe it doesn't suit me..."

"That's absolutely not true.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1216. Look how frizzy my hair is. Of course I'm envious of yours.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1217. I'd like to chop it off and use it as a wig when I'm going out."
"Ah, ahaha..."

"Have confidence, Natsumi-san.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1219. The past is the past.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1220. I'm sure nowadays everyone will tell you it's great.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1221. Have I ever lied to you?"

"And think about it.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1223. It's just a change of hairstyle, after all.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1224. So even if you do get a bad reaction, you can always just put it back the next day and reset everything."
"You could even tell them you overslept and didn't have time to put it up. Then all would be back to normal.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1226. ...But I promise you, there will be no need to say that."
"W-well... that's true, but..."

"You don't have to make any other sudden changes aside from that.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1228. Everyone around you will be surprised, of course.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1229. ...But, it's surprisingly easy to change things little by little."
"...Tomoe-san."

"And no matter how small a change it is, the world you changed with your own power will be a nicer, brighter world for you.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1231. ...That's what I believe.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1232. And I'll be praying for you."
"...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1218.Yeah."
She still seemed skeptical... but even so, Natsumi thought it over for a while and then nodded.
...She was usually very shy, and was even more likely to refuse when forcibly told to do something.
But for some reason, she felt she could completely trust Tomoe... Some sort of conviction inside her was telling her that.
...And, everything did indeed play out exactly like Tomoe had predicted.
Natsumi's faith in her was growing stronger and more certain.
"See?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1234. I wasn't lying."

"Yeah!@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1220. It's just as you said, Tomoe-san.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1221. And thanks to that, I'm starting to feel a little more confident...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1222. Thank you so much."
"Ahaha, when you put it like that it's actually embarrassing.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1236. That's the power of cuteness. It's an indisputable fact of life in any time and place."
"Th-thank you...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1224. Oh, and there's something else.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1225. ...Uhh, umm."
"Hmm? What is it?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1238. Something good?"
"Yes, very!@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1227. The truth is, umm..."
Natsumi somewhat shyly, but also very ecstatically went on to share that Akira confessed to her the other day...
"Huh? Really?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1240. Isn't that great? Congratulations!"

"Ah, ahahaha...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1229. It feels like I'm still in a dream...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1230. It was just a little change of hairstyle. I never thought something this great would happen."

"But you're able to believe it, right?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1242. From now on, I'm sure good things will happen one after another.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1243. Because you've taken a step further than you did before."
"Life may not all be good things, but I'll never accept a world where people who put in effort aren't rewarded for it. ...Right?"
"Tomoe-san...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1232. Yeah...!"
Natsumi had a smile on her face so gleeful it was about to burst, as she playfully twirled her hair around her fingers.
Just looking at that, the joy seemed to come through to Tomoe, and she began smiling herself.
(Yes... that's what I believe.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1246. I decided to believe that...)
Maybe it's just lip service. She understood that you could say anything and that would be it.
Still, if you give up on that belief... then it's all over.
And, as long as there are people who can be saved by that belief, she'll continue to carry it with her forever.
That's why Tomoe continued unwaveringly telling herself and others precisely that.
Just then, multiple waitresses began laying trays full of sweets down on the table while saying "S-sorry to keep you waiting~."
Within a moment, the area was filled with a sweet aroma... and curious gazes came pointing this way from the surrounding seats.
"Then today is a celebration♪@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1249. Natsumi-san, feel free to eat as much as you want, okay?"
"Huh?@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1234. B-but..."
"Don't worry.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1251. I was planning to order a second helping anyway."
"?!@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1236. R-really...?"
"E-eeek?!"
"Ch-chieeef!!"
The waitresses screamed and flew off toward the kitchen as soon as they heard Tomoe say that.
Shortly after they stepped inside, it seemed like pandemonium had broken out in there... but it was probably just her imagination.
"Man, is she still eating...?"
"Just what kind of stomach does she have...?"
Tomoe could hear the other customers prattling on, but she scooped up a bite of cake without any hesitation and popped it into her mouth.
By the time she finished her third helping, their curiosity had transformed into astonishment and admiration...
"Haah~.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1253. Anyway, maybe I should stop before I stuff myself?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1254. This seems like a good stopping point."
"Y-you ate every item on the menu, three times over..."
Natsumi stiffened up and turned toward the kitchen.
...The entire wait-staff was over there, looking so exhausted their souls were practically bleeding out. And the kitchen staff looked completely pale, collapsed in fatigue.
The "ladies all-you-can-eat course" will probably disappear from the Kakiuchi menu after this. ...Or maybe they'll add a caveat below it, "no professionals."
Natsumi, on the other hand, hardly ate a thing... but it was still far beyond the cost of feeding two people.
(...Hmm, maybe I'll buy some souvenirs or something to make up for it.)
Though she thought that half-teasingly, Tomoe's conscience did start to hurt, so she began staring at the take-out menu right next to her and thinking about the amount of money in her wallet.
"Oh, right.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1239. ...I need to take my medicine."
"Hmm?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1257. Have you caught a cold?"
"Oh, no...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1241. This is my regular medicine."
Natsumi pulled a small pill case out of her pouch as she said that, then opened it. Inside was a package of sickeningly vibrantly colored capsules.
There was something unsettling about the luster of the pills. ...It was a very peculiar color.

With medication, especially when taken orally... the color of the capsule is something they take into consideration. Psychologically, people have a harder time swallowing things when the coloration makes it look like a non-food item.
Nevertheless, the medicine in Natsumi's hand looked even more flashy and colorful than imported sweets.
Though she felt dubious, Tomoe also realized it could be a personal matter, so she didn't want to ask about the medication.
"...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1243.Actually, I guess it's fine?"
"What's wrong?"
"If I take this now, I'll start feeling sleepy.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1245. Tomoe-san, you're going to a lot of trouble to help me with my studying, so I'll take it after you head home."
"Is that so...?"
Is it really all right to shift the timing of taking medication...?
Those concerns ran through her head, but it was hard to imagine Natsumi suffering from some disease that could drastically change after just a few hours without medication.
If she said it was fine, then maybe it really wasn't a problem...
"The doctor told me it's like vitamins. It's okay to take a little too much or not quite enough."
"These days, I've been taking it after studying.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1248. That way my head stays clear, and I feel good."
"Wow... that's some convenient medicine.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1262. But it's bad for your body to take or rely too much on any medication, you know?"
"Yes, I'll be careful."
At any rate, Tomoe started thinking she was being overly concerned and neglecting the things right in front of her. So, she started moving the dishes to the corner of the table.
"So, were you able to finish this week's assignment?"
"Ah, yeah.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1251. I brought it with me today."
Natsumi pulled the reference book out of her bag as she said that. It was a small printed book with perforated lines for easily tearing out pages.
Tomoe spread the material out on the table in front of her. Then, she pulled a fountain pen out of her breast pocket and slowly and carefully started making corrections.

"Hmm... seems like you have a good foundation on math.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1265. English is your area of expertise, so that's no pro...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1266. Oh, these relative clauses are translated wrong. Here, take a look."
"Oh, you're right.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1253. Then if this is here... then it should be like this?"
"Yes, exactly.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1268. You'll inevitably make mistakes if you rush, so it's best to keep calm and take it slow."
"Right."
...Natsumi had been consulting with Tomoe on all sorts of things since they met at the Gogura university hospital and became friends.
At the time, she said that she was worried about her studies in school... so, Tomoe made time to help answer her questions, and at this point their meetings became like a correspondence course.

"Still though, your grasp on longer English sentences is as good as ever.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1270. It's impressive that you were able to translate these parts about the Puritan Revolution and the Bloodless Revolution so accurately."
"E-ehehe...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1256. But Tomoe-san, you're really skilled too.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1257. You can understand English text without even needing to think about it."
"Well... back in the day, I knew some people who were really obsessed with this period in England's history.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1272. I guess I ended up remembering some of it."

"Really?@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1259. I've also been a fan of British history ever since I was young.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1260. Like the legend of King Arthur, and the Age of Discovery back in Queen Elizabeth's time. It's really fun, like reading a fairy tale or a novel."
...As expected, a bookworm completely lived up to her reputation. The sparkle in her eyes as she spoke made it clear she was enjoying herself, and her voice was naturally getting heated.
"So when I go to college, I want to save up money so I can go overseas.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1262. Traveling is nice... but if I work really hard I might be able to study abroad..."
"I see.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1274. Then, are you planning to major in history or archaeology?"
"Ah, no.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1264. I actually want to study nursing in college."

"I still haven't told my parents about this, but I've been working a part-time job at a care center my friend introduced me to...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1266. And things have been going well there, so the managers recommended it to me, and I really want to do my best..."

"Wow... Isn't that nice?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1276. This is a little bit of a tangent, but Nightingale was from your beloved England, too.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1277. If you go, I'm sure you can hear all kinds of interesting stories."
...Wait, wouldn't Sweden or one of the Scandinavian countries be a better fit for nursing care?
Tomoe introspectively tilted her head, but decided to let it go for now. Interest in foreign countries in any form and visiting them is a valuable experience for gaining a wider world view.

"Well in that case, you'll have to work extra hard studying English.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1279. Next time I'll bring you some of my reference material on English conversation.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1280. They'd be really expensive to buy on your own, so feel free to use them in your spare time."
"Thanks... for everything.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1268. Even though you're so busy, you're doing so much to look out for me..."
"It's fine. I do this because I want to. No need to worry.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1282. Plus, it makes for some interesting mental gymnastics, and that makes it fun, y'know?"
"...Thanks, Tomoe-san."
"Oh, that's right.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1284. Starting this week, I'm moving back to an apartment in Kakiuchi.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1285. I'll give you my new address next time."
"Is that so?@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1271. Then..."
"Yeah.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1287. On my days off, I'll be able to meet with you one-on-one.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1288. So get ready for that, okay~?"
"Y...yeah.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1273. Looking forward to it."
Natsumi gleefully bowed her head with a smile on her face.
...Just looking at her smiling face was enough to heal all the fatigue of Tomoe's strenuous daily work and make her feel at ease.
So, Tomoe didn't feel like the time she spent with Natsumi was a burden at all.
"By the way Tomoe-san, you went to Kashima University, right?"
"Yeah, what about it?"

"When I spoke with my friends, they said Kashima University is the best college in all of Japan for people who want to become teachers.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1276. ...Somehow, that makes sense.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1277. Because you're really good at teaching things, Tomoe-san. You're like a real teacher."

"A teacher, huh...? That's a little out of character for me.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1291. I was never very good at studying.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1292. In fact, what I was seriously aiming to become was... well, I wanted to be a stewardess."
"A... stewardess?@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1279. But Tomoe-san, you were into track and field..."
"Unlike the marathon, the high jump isn't much of a corporate sport.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1294. So I didn't think I had much of a future pursuing that."
"Well, I did have dreams of going abroad when I was young, so maybe I was actually aiming for that?"
"So, my progress in English is probably a byproduct of that.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1297. I even memorized a bunch of alphabetic letter codes and technical terms."
"Letter codes?"

"Like how airports and airline companies are referred to with two or three letter acronyms.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1299. For example, Haneda Airport in HND and Kakiuchi Airport is KKU.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1300. And Japan Airlines is JA."
"They're used all across the world, so I was a little proud to be able to name all of them."
"That's amazing..."
In fact, being a police officer was the thing she most wanted to avoid. ...So she couldn't help but feel the irony of her fate.
And...
(...Reina Ryuugu.)
Talking with Natsumi and helping her study like this made Tomoe think of another girl of roughly the same age, who Tomoe was still pursuing as a material witness... or rather, a potential suspect.
Thinking about the absurdity of that juxtaposition... always made her chest ache.

"Now, you'll have to work hard and keep at it like this just a little more!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1304. The next challenge is mid-terms. Do you think you'll be able to score above average in all subjects?"
"Ah... y-yeah!@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1284. I-I'll do my best!"

"All right!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1306. ...Wait, is this really okay?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1307. Isn't this shop about to enter its rush hour?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1308. If we stay here much longer, they might get upset with us."

"Oh, it's fine.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1286. Actually, the manager of this shop is an acquaintance of mine.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1287. He's an old shogi playing partner with one of my relatives. If I asked, I'm sure it'd be okay."
"Wow...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1310. So, does this relative of yours live in Kakiuchi too?"
"No, he actually lives in a place called Hinamizawa. It's right next to Okinomiya.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1289. He's the mayor of the village there... or to be more precise, the town chairman."
...Memories from half a year ago stirred within Tomoe when she heard that explanation.
Back when she visited there with Ooishi, he told her about the "Three Families" that reigned over Hinamizawa, one of which was... the Kimiyoshi family.
And it's true that the mayor's name was Kiichirou Kimiyoshi.
"...I have my suspicions about the Three Families, which are led by the Sonozaki family."
Ooishi's words from back then came to mind... From when she was investigating the curse in Hinamizawa.
And Natsumi really did share the same blood as the Three Families...
(It was an unusual last name, so the possibility nagged at me...)
Up until now, she always wanted to ask Natsumi about it to make certain. And if she was actually connected, she'd like to ask for her help in gathering information...
Sensing a strong information source, Tomoe's thought process switched back to that of a police officer.
It wasn't just about Reina Ryuugu. Just as Ooishi said... there seemed to be something going on in Hinamizawa.
Tomoe herself was nearly killed there. But her suspicions were further heightened by the "mysterious" presence she constantly felt while on her investigation.
In the first place, the movements in the background were far too disturbing to write off as a mere murder and abnormal behavior.
And for that reason she truly wanted to get all the information she could.
(Natsumi... Kimiyoshi.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1315. A girl related to the head of the Kimiyoshi family, of the Three Families...huh?)
What should she do?
At the very least, the girl trusted Tomoe. ...And she was confident she wasn't being conceited about that notion.
So it might be possible for Tomoe to ask for her assistance, or get something out of her...
"Tomoe-san...?@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1291. Umm, Tomoe-san?"
"Huh?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1317. Wh...what is it?"
"What's wrong?@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1293. Are you feeling tired after all...?"
"No, that's not it.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1319. I was just thinking about next week's TV coverage."
"Huh?@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1295. Tomoe-san, you're going to be on TV?"
"Well, you could say that.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1321. I do hate being in the spotlight, but it's part of the job..."
She spoke with a smile on her face... but thoughts and calculations were racing inside Tomoe's head.
Right. She could just casually bring up Hinamizawa... and ask about the curse, and past incidents.
She's an honest girl, so she'd probably be willing to share everything she knew without reservations if Tomoe told her she was simply interested in folklore.
Having made up her mind, Tomoe raised her head, but just then...
"Umm... Tomoe-san."
"Hmm...? What is it?"

"I'm... an only child.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1298. I've always thought it would be nice to have an older sister like you.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1299. Someone kind and dependable, who I could talk to about anything..."

"So... umm, Tomoe-san... if you're fine with it.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1301. From now on... I was thinking it would be nice to start calling you Sis.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1302. ...Would that be all right?"
"I... I'm sorry for suddenly saying something so weird.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1304. But, I..."
Looking at that innocent, adorably pitiful expression... Tomoe immediately came to regret her distasteful thoughts from a moment ago.
You idiot... what were you thinking?!
Did you want a repeat of last time?!
Did you want her to end up like that other girl?!
"Minai-san."
She recalled the humble smile of a girl who loved flowers.
...Tomoe got too heavily involved with her... and then she lost her life.
If Tomoe hadn't gotten her connected to the series of murders in Hinamizawa, she'd be living a peaceful life right now...
(That girl's future was robbed from her... by me.)
Even now, every time she saw a long-haired girl passing by... it brought back memories of their conversation back then.
Carelessly getting people involved with the series of deaths in Hinamizawa... is literally putting their lives at risk.
...Tomoe had had enough of that.
She didn't want to see another innocent, blameless child with a right to be happy suffering misfortune.

"...Ah, ahahaha.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1328. I'd love to have a little sister as cute as you, Natsumi-san.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1329. Well, my actual little sister is very different from you. Kind of mean spirited and, how should I put it...?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1330. She'd benefit from following your example! That's what I think~."
"Huh? Is that so?"
"Hmm, but, well... thinking about it now, that's probably the reason she wasn't popular with the boys."

"She received love letters from the younger girls, but the boys were always too scared to come close.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1333. And makes faces that say "your tie is crooked, y'know?"@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1334. I, on the other hand, was completely normal!"
"Ahahahah, ahahahaha..."
Natsumi responded to that joke with roaring laughter.
...Tomoe recently noticed that Natsumi had been trying really hard to listen to other people and have fun. So, she seemed to be really enjoying the conversation.
(...I don't want to betray her.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1336. Never again...)
Tomoe herself realized she was being soft.
Still... she didn't want to get other people involved and invite further misfortune.
"...Thank you, Minai-san."
Looking at Natsumi's long, straight hair.
For a moment, a vision of that girl overlapped the scene... so Tomoe gently turned away.
Was she trying to get close to Natsumi to atone for what happened to her...?
At the moment... Tomoe didn't have the confidence to say otherwise.
"Anyway... it's gotten really late.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1339. Are you sure this is okay?"

"Oh, yeah.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1308. I told my parents I was out studying with an older friend.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1309. I already let them know I was studying at Angel Mort, and I can show them the study materials as evidence. So it'll be fine."
"...Oh really?"
At any rate... it's normal for parents to be concerned about a girl her age going out, but what kind of parents demands their child present evidence?
Even in a place like this, signs of Natsumi's stifling home life were on full display...
"Then, why don't you take a taxi with me?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1342. It's on the way back to my apartment, so the fare is the same either way."
"Ah, but..."
"It's fine.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1344. Leave it to your older sister... okay?"
"Ahaha... Then, I'll have to accept your kind offer."
The cityscape was flowing quietly outside the window.
There were so many people on the roads downtown, even though it was still sparse compared to the daytime.
Though there were streetlights along the way, the path was more than overwhelmed by the darkness. Clearly an uncomfortable place for a girl to be walking alone.
(I guess a taxi was the right call.)
While muttering that to herself, Tomoe turned toward Natsumi sitting beside her and smiled. She was quietly snoring, sleeping peacefully.
"...Zzz, zzz..."
Though she was hesitant to do so when she first entered the backseat... within minutes of the drive, she fell asleep leaning against Tomoe's shoulder.
She took her regular medicine right before they left Angel Mort, so that may have been a contributing factor. Even when the car lightly shook, she showed no signs of awakening.
"She must be tired..."
She gently placed a hand on Natsumi's shoulder and ran her fingers through her long hair.

Even though she was getting a little more accustomed to it, she was living a busy city life... while also studying for exams. No matter how hard she tried, it wouldn't be strange at all for her to be tired.
And on top of that, Natsumi's mother had heard about her struggles in school. It would be difficult to find a moment to rest under that sort of pressure.
"Huh?"
Natsumi faintly muttered that in her sleep, while her cheek was resting against her shoulder.
The occasional light from the bright streetlights and neon signs revealed her calm sleeping face, which was very lovely for someone her age.
(...It's strange.)
Tomoe was used to being looked up to for her caring nature... but she'd only known Natsumi for half a year now.

And yet, she hadn't opened her heart to anyone like this apart from the sister she'd been taking care of since she was a child. She was a little surprised that she could get close enough to fawn over someone like this.

And for some reason, she was a bit disconcerted by the part of her that was naturally able to accept it. She knew, in consideration of her job, she should've refrained from getting too close, and yet...
But as strange as that feeling was, she couldn't say she felt unpleasant.
In fact, she was delighted... and very comforted.
